steps.title: Block the overall shape (pencil)
steps.description: Make sure you're in pencil mode. Lightly sketch a flattened oval for the outer edge of the coil — wider than it is tall, as if you're looking down at it at an angle.

steps.title: Sketch the inner hole (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, add a smaller flattened oval near the middle of the first, slightly off-center. This is the hole at the center of the coil where the windings meet.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the outer edge
steps.description: Switch to ink. Lay down the outer oval in one confident pass. A slight wobble in the line gives the rope a hand-laid feel.

steps.title: Ink the inner windings
steps.description: Still in ink, draw two or three concentric ovals working inward from the outer edge, each one tucked just inside the last. They don't have to be perfectly spaced — uneven windings look more like real rope.

steps.title: Add the loose end (ink)
steps.description: Draw a short curved line peeling off the outer edge of the coil, like the end of the rope coming free. A small fray mark at the tip — three or four tiny strokes — finishes it.

steps.title: Clean up
steps.description: Switch to the eraser and remove any pencil guides that distract from the ink. Embrace the unevenness — a too-perfect coil looks like a target, not rope.
